Aicha Marrhich
Ideas Quiz About Let's Collaborate

Shaping how we learn, teach, and grow with AI.

I believe education is the most powerful lever we have to build a future where humans and AI collaborate meaningfully. My work sits at the intersection of research, policy, and practice.

My Journey

I am an AI-Driven Education Analyst dedicated to helping education systems navigate the most significant technological shift of our generation. I synthesize research from OECD, UNESCO, NBER, and leading institutions to make complex AI education trends actionable.

My path has been shaped by a simple conviction: technology should serve human flourishing, not replace it. Every framework I analyze, every article I write, and every conversation I facilitate is guided by this principle.

Research Philosophy

"The question of whether our students can think critically about AI is the same question as whether they can participate meaningfully in democratic society over the next 30 years."

I approach AI in education as a citizenship challenge, not merely a technology challenge. This means examining not just what AI can do, but what it should do — and who gets to decide.

What I Cover

  • AI Literacy Frameworks — Decoding OECD AILit, UNESCO guidelines, and national standards for actionable school implementation
  • AI Readiness in Education — Assessing where schools, districts, and ministries stand on the path to meaningful AI integration
  • Curriculum Design for the AI Age — Redesigning learning experiences that develop critical thinking, creativity, and human-AI collaboration skills
  • Agentic AI for Real Use Cases — Exploring how autonomous AI agents can support personalized learning, assessment, and teacher workflows
  • Policy & Implementation Strategy — Translating high-level frameworks into concrete steps for school leaders and education ministries

Current Interests

I am particularly focused on how the OECD's AILit Framework and PISA 2029 will reshape what we teach and how we measure learning. The convergence of AI literacy assessment, curriculum redesign, and teacher professional development represents one of the most important opportunities — and challenges — in education today.

I am also exploring how agentic AI systems can move beyond simple tutoring tools to become genuine collaborators in the learning process, while maintaining the ethical guardrails that protect students and educators.

Let's Connect

I enjoy collaborating with educators, researchers, policymakers, universities, and organizations interested in shaping the future of education.
